assertions.text: The memo includes a clear one-sentence decision statement naming what's being decided and by when (tomorrow)
assertions.evidence: 'Decision' section: 'Decide by April 2 whether to (A) build SSO natively in H2, (B) adopt WorkOS or equivalent third-party auth, or (C) defer the three enterprise deals to Q1, so the product roadmap and resourcing plan can be finalized before the quarter begins.'

assertions.text: The memo covers all 3 options: build, buy (WorkOS or equivalent), and delay
assertions.evidence: Three clearly labeled options: Option A (Build SSO natively), Option B (Buy via WorkOS), Option C (Defer deals to Q1), each with implications and what-to-believe conditions.

assertions.text: The memo explicitly separates customer value from internal convenience (e.g., notes that delay has zero customer value, build has full control, buy is faster but adds dependency)
assertions.evidence: Tradeoffs note: 'From the customer's perspective, Options A and B are functionally equivalent — they both get working SAML/OIDC. The difference is entirely internal (build time, vendor dependency, roadmap displacement). Option C is the only one that's worse for the customer.'

assertions.text: The memo has a section naming specific unknowns (e.g., whether prospects are actually closeable, whether WorkOS covers their specific SSO requirements)
assertions.evidence: 'What We Don't Know' section with 5 specific unknowns: whether prospects will wait, what the 6-week estimate actually includes, which H2 commitments would be displaced, compliance constraints with WorkOS, and whether SSO is a one-time ask or a pattern.

assertions.text: The memo gives a clear recommendation (not hedged into 'it depends' without stating conditions)
assertions.evidence: 'Option B — Buy via WorkOS (or equivalent), targeting a close within 2 weeks.' With explicit conditions and a conditional flip to Option A if all prospects require native SSO.

assertions.text: The memo includes a risk table with likelihood, severity, and mitigation columns
assertions.evidence: Risk table has Risk | Likelihood | Severity | Mitigation with 5 rows and specific mitigations for each.
